steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by stating volunteering benefits society as a whole, then shows a survey where most youths see only individual benefits — and only 6% recognize societal value. The gap between actual societal impact and youth perception drives the correct answer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — many young people think they can volunteer only within their own communities.: The passage says nothing about where volunteering opportunities exist. ✗
  • Choice B — volunteering may be even more helpful than many young people think it is.: Volunteering's societal value is established upfront, but most youths miss it — so it is more helpful than they realize. ✓
  • Choice C — volunteering can help society overall more than it can help individual people.: The passage doesn't compare how much individual versus societal help volunteering provides. ✗
  • Choice D — many young people may not know how to find ways to volunteer their time.: The survey addresses perception of benefits, not whether youths can find volunteer work. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
